title: European stock markets reach historic highs with DAX near 26,000 points and Ibex above 19,800
summary: European stock exchanges extend their rally, encouraged by geopolitical détente and strong employment data in the United States.

title: Lockheed bids for Ultra Maritime, Alphabet invested in SpaceX, Jersey Mike's prepares to go public, and Apple reacts to the market
summary: Global corporate activity accelerates with major deals in defense, space technology, and consumer sectors, while Blackstone monetizes its portfolio and retail investors bet on space and defense.

title: Spain authorizes 'stablecoin' payments and CNMV updates crypto-asset regulations as digital euro progresses in Europe
summary: The Bank of Spain greenlights Crossmint, the CNMV includes cryptocurrencies in its securities regulation, the ECB promotes the digital euro, and the British FCA criticizes the opacity of investment firms' financial documents.

title: The rise of artificial intelligence polarizes the market between investor optimism and fear of an inflationary bubble
summary: While semiconductor demand boosts Micron and AMD, analysts debate the impact of AI on inflation and retail investors warn of possible manipulation by large funds.

title: Strong US employment data curbs recession fears but fuels debate over a profit bubble and Trump's criticism of the Fed
summary: The June employment report dispels recession fears; however, record valuations on Wall Street and Donald Trump's attacks on the Federal Reserve create uncertainty among investors.

title: Apollo and Ardian face difficulties in their portfolio companies, Julius Baer renews its financial leadership, and Tanabe Pharma sells its manufacturing subsidiary
summary: Private equity firms deal with problems in their supermarket and healthcare investments, the Swiss bank appoints a new CFO, and the Japanese pharmaceutical company restructures to focus on R&D.

title: E-commerce breaks records in Spain with €114.8 billion in revenue, Ryanair attacks French air traffic control, and Spanish Tax Agency refunds €9.361 billion
summary: Online sales grow 20.6% year-on-year, the Irish airline estimates the cost of air traffic controller strikes in France at €1.7 billion, the tax return campaign ends with record refunds, and the United States celebrates its 250th anniversary amid reflections on wealth.

title: Lagarde opens the door to an early departure from the ECB and defends the rate hike as a 'correct' decision
summary: The President of the European Central Bank supports her legacy of restrictive monetary policy and does not rule out leaving the institution before the end of her term.

title: The truce between the United States and Iran increases traffic in the Strait of Hormuz and relieves pressure on oil, gold, and bitcoin
summary: The ceasefire changes commodity outlook: Brent crude could fall to $60, gold seeks direction, and bitcoin tries to recover $62,000.

title: Colonial enters the German real estate market, Ercros receives a delisting takeover bid, and Securitize debuts on Wall Street with an 11% rise
summary: The Spanish real estate company acquires 51% of Generali Real Estate, the CNMV admits Bondalti's takeover bid for Ercros, and the first Spanish unicorn on the Nasdaq celebrates a successful IPO.

title: Investors seek refuge in dividends and growth stocks: Realty Income, Repsol, Comcast, and companies reaching target prices
summary: Passive investment and recurring income strategies gain prominence, with companies like PPG, KMB, and PAYX exceeding analysts' average valuations in a bullish market environment.

title: Barclays identifies bargains in automation, Morningstar highlights Ferrovial among its global favorites, and insider trades mark the session
summary: Barclays analysts recommend robotics stocks after the sector correction, Silver Alpha management company launches to market, and insider buying and selling in listed companies capture investor interest.

title: Trump intensifies his criticism of the Federal Reserve and defends his family's businesses amid geopolitical détente with Iran
summary: The former US president attacks the Fed's monetary policy and justifies his children's business activities, while the ceasefire in the Middle East reduces tensions in the energy market.

title: Stellantis increases its production in Italy by 13.7% but falls on the stock market, while Siemens rises strongly and Ford outsources its batteries to CATL
summary: The automotive sector shows mixed results: Stellantis' production grows although the market punishes the stock, Siemens appreciates, and Ford hands over management of its battery plant to Chinese giant CATL.

title: Sandisk surges more than 6,000% since its spinoff from Western Digital and investors debate whether it's time to sell or hold
summary: The memory chip maker's stock captures attention due to its spectacular rise and possible stock split, while investment forums discuss whether the stock is overbought or still has potential.

title: Asian markets rise on strong US employment data while Japan debates tax reform and China authorizes Unitree's IPO
summary: The Nikkei and Kospi trade higher, Japan assesses the impact of its fiscal deficit and reduction of hospital beds, and Chinese robotics company Unitree gets the green light for its long-awaited IPO.

title: ArcelorMittal and Acerinox lead the Ibex 35 gains in a day of contrasts with declines in Rovi, Amadeus, and IAG
summary: The steel sector boosts the Spanish selective index at mid-session, while technology, pharmaceutical, and tourism stocks correct; Ferrovial is highlighted by Morningstar and Silver Alpha begins its journey as an independent management company.

title: Retirement abroad, Nigel Farage's gold business, and the evolution of wealth in the United States focus investors' reflections
summary: Handelsblatt analyzes the best tools for planning retirement outside Germany, the FCA examines the marketing of gold bars by the British politician, and the Financial Times reflects on the meaning of wealth on the 250th anniversary of the US.

title: Japan prepares for local elections, traditional festivals, and the opening of new tourist routes while Terumo looks to the United States
summary: Japanese current affairs combine tradition and business: from the 'Abare Matsuri' in Noto and the opening of the Kurobe Gorge, to the election campaign in Gunma and Terumo's expansion plans in the US CDMO market.

title: Euribor falls and approaches the 2.7% threshold, easing the financial burden of variable-rate mortgages in Spain
summary: The main reference indicator for mortgage loans in Spain extends its downward trend, in line with expectations of further interest rate cuts by the European Central Bank.
